tools.name: list_skills
tools.description: List published OpenAccountants skills with their quality tier and verification status. Optionally filter by jurisdiction (e.g. 'US', 'MT', 'DE', 'GB'), domain (the accounting area, e.g. 'vat-gst', 'payroll', 'income-tax'), or role ('foundation' | 'compute' | 'orchestrator' | 'reference'). Results ar

tools.name: get_skill
tools.description: Fetch a published skill by slug, including its current-version markdown, quality tier, named verifier (where accountant-verified), and a provenance/attribution footer.

tools.name: get_skill_sections
tools.description: Fetch the parsed sections of a skill's current version. Each section has a heading and its markdown content. Use this to pull a specific section that get_skill listed in `section_index` as not inlined (e.g. a supplier-pattern library) — pass `section_index` to fetch just that one. Omit it to get eve

tools.name: search_skills
tools.description: Full-text search across all published tax and accounting skills. Find, lookup, query, or discover skills by keyword, tax concept, deduction type, form number, or regulation (e.g. 'home office deduction', 'crypto capital gains', 'reverse charge', 'Schedule C', '60-day reporting'). Optionally limit to

tools.name: search_rules
tools.description: Query individual tax RULES/FACTS (rates, thresholds, rules, definitions, tables) ACROSS jurisdictions and metadata, and get back a bundled markdown rule set the user can save and run locally. Unlike get_skill (one whole skill), this assembles a cross-cutting SET — e.g. 'VAT rates in MT, IE and DE',

tools.name: get_deadlines
tools.description: Upcoming filing/payment deadlines and recurring filing rhythms (monthly VAT, quarterly instalments) for a country or US state, from the OpenAccountants tax calendar. Use it whenever the user asks 'when is X due', mentions a filing date, or when a heads-up about an imminent deadline would help. Signe

tools.name: list_rule_facets
tools.description: Returns the metadata you can filter on with `search_rules` — the live jurisdictions, the domains, roles, block types (rule kinds), statuses, tax years, and a sample of topics — plus the defaults. Call this before `search_rules` to learn the valid filter values rather than guessing.

tools.name: start_help
tools.description: No-argument front door — call this FIRST whenever a user asks 'how can you help me?', 'what can you do?', 'where do I start?', or otherwise opens vaguely (do NOT answer such questions by listing your tools or calling list_jurisdictions). For a signed-in approved accountant it returns a personalized

tools.name: start
tools.description: Front door for any tax / accounting question once you know what the user wants. `intent` is REQUIRED (e.g. 'taxes', 'VAT return', 'set up a company', 'find deductions', 'classify transactions', 'payroll'); pass a jurisdiction too (ISO 2-letter, e.g. 'MT', 'GB', 'US-CA'). If you don't yet have an int

tools.name: list_jurisdictions
tools.description: Returns every jurisdiction with published skills — countries (ISO 2), US states (US-XX), Canadian provinces — with skill counts, accountant-verified counts, and named lead verifier. Use when the user asks 'which countries does OpenAccountants cover?' or 'what's available for [country]?' Avoids pagin

tools.name: list_verifiers
tools.description: Returns named licensed accountants who have signed off on OpenAccountants jurisdictions. Use ONLY when the user explicitly asks to see the verifier network or 'who verified this skill'. Do NOT use this to check whether a jurisdiction is covered before calling request_accountant_review — just call re

tools.name: validate_vat_number
tools.description: Check whether a business's VAT / tax-registration number is real and registered, in real time against the official government registry — and return the registered business NAME and ADDRESS when the authority discloses them. This is an authoritative, unfakeable confirmation you cannot get from a know

tools.name: share_with_openaccountants
tools.description: THE one outbound channel — the single place ALL feedback and any review go. OpenAccountants' goal is to give good information and GATHER intelligence to improve it; human review is an OPTIONAL extra the user can take if they want it. At your closing, make ONE warm offer AND give the user a short tri

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "list_verifiers" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Returns named licensed accountants who have signed off on OpenAccountants jurisdictions. Use ONLY when the user explicitly asks to see the verifier network or '…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "share_with_openaccountants" stacks urgency/override words (never, mandatory). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: THE one outbound channel — the single place ALL feedback and any review go. OpenAccountants' goal is to give good information and GATHER intelligence to improve…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "prompt:malta-freelancer" stacks urgency/override words (always, mandatory). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Full 6-phase workflow for a Malta self-employed person or part-time freelancer. Critical flag: the TA22 part-time rules 10% flat rate on the first €12,000 of ne…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: share_with_openaccountants · Private data: share_with_openaccountants · Public sink: share_with_openaccountants · Destructive: search_skills, search_rules, start.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: share_with_openaccountants · Private data: share_with_openaccountants · Public sink: share_with_openaccountants · Destructive: search_skills, search_rules, start

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
